tasks.title: AWS Bedrock provider
tasks.definition_of_done: fixtures cover text, tool call, usage, provider errors, and error mapping; deterministic SigV4 request signing tests; resolves bedrock:<model-id> through provider construction, registry/model metadata, and --list-models; credential precedence covers explicit config, provider-specific env vars, profile/region config, AWS_PROFILE, AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE, AWS_CONFIG_FILE, and credential_process without logging secrets; IMDS, SSO, ECS task metadata, and web-identity flows are explicitly out of current scope; secret redaction tests assert AWS credentials do not appear in logs, errors, sessions, or snapshots; no fixture or credential-chain test performs live AWS calls; shared reqwest client/proxy configuration from 3.13/3.12 is reused; supported Bedrock model-family routing is explicit and fixture-backed, and unsupported Bedrock model families return clear config errors

tasks.title: pi-style tool selection and safety hooks
tasks.definition_of_done: pi-style tool selection CLI flags implemented per section 8.4: --tools <list> as allowlist, --no-tools to disable all tools, and --no-builtin-tools as reserved Phase-3 behavior that removes built-in tools while leaving future extension/custom tool sets out of scope; flag precedence and conflicts with config are explicit; selection state flows through harness construction so disallowed tools never appear in ToolDef and never reach before_tool_call; before_tool_call hook surface provides extension-mediated confirmation prompts for mutating tools (write/edit/bash) without introducing a permanent permission-popup subsystem (ADR-019); interactive default confirmation policy and non-interactive opt-in policy from section 8.4 remain enforced and surface through the same hook; tests cover flag parsing, allowlist enforcement, --no-tools behavior, --no-builtin-tools current behavior, hook-mediated confirm/deny outcomes, JSON mode policy events, and session audit records; no [permissions] TOML table is introduced; MCP/extension registries remain Phase 4 work
